---
title: "Threading Speed Test"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Threading Speed Test}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}

---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```
For testing if multi-threading speeds up stochastic simulations

```{r setup}
library(multigroup.vaccine)
```

Large population:

```{r}
popsize <- c(800000, 200000)
```

Generic simulation settings - expect long simulations with R0 = 2 and no immunity
```{r}
relcontact <- c(1, 1.7)
incontact <- c(0.2, 0.5)
contactmatrix <- contactMatrixPropPref(popsize, relcontact, incontact)
relsusc <- c(1, 1.05)
reltransm <- c(1, 1.01)
R0 <- 4
initR <- c(0, 0)
initI <- c(0, 1)
initV <- c(0, 0)
```

Final outbreak size (stochastic method)

Time of simulation with 1 thread:
```{r}
tm1 <- Sys.time()
fs <- finalsize(popsize, R0, contactmatrix, relsusc, reltransm, initR, initI, initV,
                method = "stochastic", nsims = 100, nthreads = 1)
Sys.time() - tm1
```

Time of simulation with 2 threads:
```{r}
tm2 <- Sys.time()
fs <- finalsize(popsize, R0, contactmatrix, relsusc, reltransm, initR, initI, initV,
                method = "stochastic", nsims = 100, nthreads = 2)
Sys.time() - tm2
```

Time of simulation with 3 threads:
```{r}
tm3 <- Sys.time()
fs <- finalsize(popsize, R0, contactmatrix, relsusc, reltransm, initR, initI, initV,
                method = "stochastic", nsims = 100, nthreads = 3)
Sys.time() - tm3
```
